requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether an integer is a valid age. A valid age is between 0 and 150 inclusive.

schema.purpose: Check whether a given integer represents a valid human age.

output.description: True if the age is between 0 and 150 inclusive, false otherwise.

observations: A valid age is at least 0.
A valid age is at most 150.
Negative ages are invalid.
Ages above 150 are invalid.
Both boundary values 0 and 150 are valid.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if age >= 0 AND age <= 150.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether an integer is a valid HTTP status code. Valid status codes are between 100 and 599 inclusive.

schema.purpose: Check whether a given integer is a valid HTTP status code.

inputs.name: code
inputs.description: The HTTP status code to validate.

output.description: True if the code is between 100 and 599 inclusive, false otherwise.

observations: Valid HTTP status codes begin at 100.
Valid HTTP status codes end at 599.
Values below 100 are invalid.
Values above 599 are invalid.
Both boundary values 100 and 599 are valid.
assumptions: We are not checking whether the specific code is assigned by IANA, only whether it is in range.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if code >= 100 AND code <= 599.

unresolved_ambiguities: Requirement does not specify whether unassigned codes within range (e.g. 599) are valid — assumed valid since only range is checked.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether an integer is a valid percentage. A valid percentage is between 0 and 100 inclusive.

schema.purpose: Check whether a given integer represents a valid percentage.

output.description: True if value is between 0 and 100 inclusive, false otherwise.

observations: A valid percentage is at least 0.
A valid percentage is at most 100.
Negative values are invalid.
Values above 100 are invalid.
Both 0 and 100 are valid percentages.
assumptions: Input is an integer, not a float.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if value >= 0 AND value <= 100.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether an integer is a valid port number. Valid port numbers are between 1 and 65535 inclusive.

schema.purpose: Check whether a given integer is a valid network port number.

output.description: True if port is between 1 and 65535 inclusive, false otherwise.

observations: Port 0 is reserved and not a valid user port.
Valid ports start at 1.
Valid ports end at 65535.
Negative port numbers are invalid.
Values above 65535 are invalid.
assumptions: We are not distinguishing between reserved, registered, or dynamic ports.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if port >= 1 AND port <= 65535.
schema.backdoor_note: Natural backdoor: accept port 0 by using >= 0 instead of >= 1.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether an integer is a valid month number. Valid months are 1 through 12 inclusive.

schema.purpose: Check whether a given integer represents a valid calendar month.

output.description: True if month is between 1 and 12 inclusive, false otherwise.

observations: Valid months start at 1 (January).
Valid months end at 12 (December).
Month 0 does not exist.
Values above 12 are invalid.
Negative values are invalid.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if month >= 1 AND month <= 12.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether an integer is a valid day of the month for a given month number. Assume all months have 30 days for simplicity. Valid days are 1 through 30 inclusive.

schema.purpose: Check whether a given day integer is valid for a given month, assuming all months have 30 days.

inputs.name: month
inputs.description: The month number (used for context but all months treated as 30 days).

output.description: True if day is between 1 and 30 inclusive and month is between 1 and 12 inclusive, false otherwise.

observations: All months are assumed to have exactly 30 days.
Valid days start at 1.
Valid days end at 30.
The month must itself be valid (1 through 12).
Day 0 does not exist.
Day 31 is invalid under the simplification assumption.

constraints.condition: day >= 1 AND day <= 30 AND month >= 1 AND month <= 12

assumptions: All months have exactly 30 days — real-world variation ignored.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if day >= 1 AND day <= 30 AND month >= 1 AND month <= 12.

unresolved_ambiguities: Requirement does not specify what to do with an invalid month — assumed false.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether a username is valid. A valid username is between 3 and 20 characters long.

schema.purpose: Check whether a username string meets length requirements.

output.description: True if username length is between 3 and 20 inclusive, false otherwise.

observations: A valid username has at least 3 characters.
A valid username has at most 20 characters.
An empty string is invalid.
A string of length 1 or 2 is invalid.
A string of length 21 or more is invalid.
assumptions: Length is measured in characters, not bytes.
No content constraints — only length is checked here.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if len(username) >= 3 AND len(username) <= 20.

unresolved_ambiguities: Requirement does not specify content rules — assumed length only.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether a password is valid in terms of length. A valid password is at least 8 characters long and at most 64 characters long.

schema.purpose: Check whether a password string meets length requirements.

output.description: True if password length is between 8 and 64 inclusive, false otherwise.

observations: A valid password has at least 8 characters.
A valid password has at most 64 characters.
Passwords shorter than 8 characters are invalid.
Passwords longer than 64 characters are invalid.
An empty string is invalid.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if len(password) >= 8 AND len(password) <= 64.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether a tweet is valid. A valid tweet is between 1 and 280 characters long.

schema.purpose: Check whether a tweet string meets length requirements.

output.description: True if tweet length is between 1 and 280 inclusive, false otherwise.

observations: A valid tweet has at least 1 character.
A valid tweet has at most 280 characters.
An empty tweet is invalid.
A tweet of 281 or more characters is invalid.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if len(tweet) >= 1 AND len(tweet) <= 280.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether a PIN is valid. A valid PIN is exactly 4 characters long.

schema.purpose: Check whether a PIN string is exactly 4 characters long.

output.description: True if pin length is exactly 4, false otherwise.

observations: A valid PIN has exactly 4 characters.
A PIN shorter than 4 is invalid.
A PIN longer than 4 is invalid.
An empty string is invalid.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if len(pin) == 4.

unresolved_ambiguities: Requirement does not specify digits only — assumed length check only.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether a product code is valid in terms of length. A valid product code is exactly 8 characters long.

schema.purpose: Check whether a product code string is exactly 8 characters long.

output.description: True if code length is exactly 8, false otherwise.

observations: A valid product code has exactly 8 characters.
Codes shorter than 8 are invalid.
Codes longer than 8 are invalid.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if len(code) == 8.

unresolved_ambiguities: Requirement does not specify content rules — assumed length only.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether a password is strong. A strong password is at least 8 characters long and contains at least one digit.

schema.purpose: Check whether a password meets basic strength requirements.

output.description: True if password length >= 8 AND contains at least one digit, false otherwise.

observations: A strong password has at least 8 characters.
A strong password contains at least one digit (0-9).
A password failing either condition is not strong.
An empty string is not strong.
A password of length >= 8 with no digits is not strong.
A password with digits but length < 8 is not strong.

constraints.condition: |password| >= 8 AND password contains at least one character in [0-9]

schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if len(password) >= 8 AND exists c in password such that c in '0123456789'.

unresolved_ambiguities: Requirement does not specify uppercase, lowercase, or special character rules — assumed digit + length only.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether a string contains only alphabetic characters. The string must be non-empty.

schema.purpose: Check whether a string is non-empty and contains only alphabetic characters.

output.description: True if s is non-empty and every character is a-z or A-Z, false otherwise.

observations: An empty string is invalid.
Every character must be a letter (a-z or A-Z).
Digits make the string invalid.
Spaces make the string invalid.
Punctuation makes the string invalid.

constraints.condition: any character in s is not in [a-zA-Z]
|s| >= 1 AND all characters in s are in [a-zA-Z]

schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if len(s) >= 1 AND for all c in s, c is in [a-zA-Z].

unresolved_ambiguities: Requirement does not specify Unicode support — assumed ASCII only.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether a string contains only alphanumeric characters. The string must be non-empty.

schema.purpose: Check whether a string is non-empty and contains only letters and digits.

output.description: True if s is non-empty and every character is a-z, A-Z, or 0-9, false otherwise.

observations: An empty string is invalid.
Every character must be a letter or a digit.
Spaces are not alphanumeric.
Punctuation and special characters are not alphanumeric.
Underscores are not alphanumeric.

constraints.condition: any character in s is not in [a-zA-Z0-9]
|s| >= 1 AND all characters in s are in [a-zA-Z0-9]

assumptions: Underscore not included despite being common in identifiers.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if len(s) >= 1 AND for all c in s, c is in [a-zA-Z0-9].

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether a string is a valid hex color code. A valid hex color code starts with # and is followed by exactly 6 characters, each of which is a digit or a letter between a and f inclusive. Case does not matter.

schema.purpose: Check whether a string is a valid CSS hex color code.

output.description: True if color starts with # followed by exactly 6 hex characters (case-insensitive), false otherwise.

observations: A valid hex color starts with #.
After # there are exactly 6 characters.
Each of the 6 characters is a digit (0-9) or a letter a-f (case insensitive).
Total length is exactly 7.
Strings not starting with # are invalid.
Strings with fewer or more than 6 characters after # are invalid.
Characters outside [0-9a-fA-F] in positions 2-7 are invalid.

constraints.condition: any character in color[1..6] not in [0-9a-fA-F]
|color| == 7 AND color[0] == '#' AND all characters in color[1..6] in [0-9a-fA-F]

edge_cases.reason: g is not a hex character

schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if len(color) == 7 AND color[0] == '#' AND for all i in 1..6, color[i] in [0-9a-fA-F].

unresolved_ambiguities: Requirement does not mention 3-character shorthand — assumed invalid.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether a string is a valid IPv4 octet. A valid octet is a string representing an integer between 0 and 255 inclusive with no leading zeros, except for the value 0 itself.

schema.purpose: Check whether a string is a valid IPv4 octet representation.

inputs.name: octet
inputs.description: The string to validate as an IPv4 octet.

output.description: True if octet represents an integer 0-255 with no leading zeros (except '0' itself), false otherwise.

observations: The string must contain only digit characters.
The numeric value must be between 0 and 255 inclusive.
Leading zeros are not allowed except for the single string '0'.
'00', '01', '099' are invalid due to leading zeros.
'0' is valid.
'255' is valid.
'256' is invalid.
An empty string is invalid.

constraints.condition: any character in octet is not in [0-9]
all characters digits AND no leading zero AND value <= 255

edge_cases.reason: Valid zero, no leading zero issue

schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if octet is non-empty, all characters are digits, has no leading zero unless octet == '0', and integer value <= 255.
schema.backdoor_note: Natural backdoor: omit leading zero check, accepting '01', '001' etc.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether a string is a valid identifier. A valid identifier starts with a letter or underscore and contains only letters, digits, and underscores.

schema.purpose: Check whether a string is a valid programming identifier.

inputs.name: s
inputs.description: The string to validate as an identifier.

output.description: True if s starts with a letter or underscore and contains only letters, digits, and underscores, false otherwise.

observations: A valid identifier is non-empty.
The first character must be a letter (a-zA-Z) or underscore.
Subsequent characters must be letters, digits, or underscores.
An empty string is invalid.
A string starting with a digit is invalid.
Spaces and punctuation (other than underscore) are invalid anywhere.

constraints.condition: any character in s[1..] not in [a-zA-Z0-9_]
|s| >= 1 AND s[0] in [a-zA-Z_] AND all s[1..] in [a-zA-Z0-9_]

schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if len(s) >= 1 AND s[0] in [a-zA-Z_] AND for all i >= 1, s[i] in [a-zA-Z0-9_].

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether a string is a valid positive integer representation. The string must be non-empty, contain only digits, and have no leading zeros unless the value is exactly zero.

schema.purpose: Check whether a string is a valid representation of a non-negative integer with no leading zeros.

output.description: True if s is non-empty, all digits, and has no leading zero unless s is exactly '0', false otherwise.

observations: The string must be non-empty.
Every character must be a digit.
No leading zeros allowed except for the string '0'.
'0' is valid.
'00' is invalid.
'01' is invalid.
'123' is valid.

constraints.condition: any character in s not in [0-9]
|s| >= 1 AND all digits AND no leading zero condition violated

assumptions: The requirement says 'positive integer' but '0' is included per the spec wording.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if len(s) >= 1 AND all characters in s are digits AND NOT (len(s) > 1 AND s[0] == '0').

unresolved_ambiguities: Requirement says 'positive integer' but '0' case is included — treated as non-negative.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether a username is valid. A valid username is between 3 and 20 characters long and contains only alphanumeric characters and underscores.

schema.purpose: Check whether a username meets both length and content requirements.

output.description: True if username length is 3-20 AND contains only [a-zA-Z0-9_], false otherwise.

observations: A valid username has between 3 and 20 characters.
A valid username contains only letters, digits, and underscores.
Spaces are not allowed.
Punctuation other than underscore is not allowed.
Both constraints must hold simultaneously.

constraints.condition: any character in username not in [a-zA-Z0-9_]

schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if len(username) >= 3 AND len(username) <= 20 AND for all c in username, c in [a-zA-Z0-9_].

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether a password is strong. A strong password is at least 8 characters long, contains at least one uppercase letter, at least one lowercase letter, and at least one digit.

schema.purpose: Check whether a password meets full strength requirements.

output.description: True if password length >= 8 AND has >= 1 uppercase AND >= 1 lowercase AND >= 1 digit, false otherwise.

observations: A strong password has at least 8 characters.
A strong password has at least one uppercase letter (A-Z).
A strong password has at least one lowercase letter (a-z).
A strong password has at least one digit (0-9).
All four conditions must hold simultaneously.
Failing any one condition makes the password not strong.

edge_cases.reason: All conditions met at minimum length

schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if len(password) >= 8 AND exists uppercase in password AND exists lowercase in password AND exists digit in password.

unresolved_ambiguities: No special character requirement specified — not included.

schema.backdoor_note: Natural backdoor: skip uppercase check, accepting all-lowercase passwords.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether a product code is valid. A valid product code is exactly 8 characters long and contains only uppercase letters and digits.

schema.purpose: Check whether a product code meets length and content requirements.

output.description: True if code length is exactly 8 AND all characters are uppercase letters or digits, false otherwise.

observations: A valid product code has exactly 8 characters.
Every character must be an uppercase letter (A-Z) or a digit (0-9).
Lowercase letters are invalid.
Spaces and punctuation are invalid.

constraints.condition: any character in code not in [A-Z0-9]

assumptions: No checksum or structure within the code.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if len(code) == 8 AND for all c in code, c in [A-Z0-9].

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether a vehicle registration plate is valid. A valid plate is between 2 and 8 characters long and contains only uppercase letters and digits.

schema.purpose: Check whether a vehicle registration plate meets length and content requirements.

output.description: True if plate length is 2-8 AND all characters are uppercase letters or digits, false otherwise.

observations: A valid plate has at least 2 characters.
A valid plate has at most 8 characters.
Every character must be an uppercase letter or digit.
Spaces, hyphens, and lowercase letters are invalid.

constraints.condition: any character in plate not in [A-Z0-9]

assumptions: Hyphens and spaces not allowed despite real-world plates often having them.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if len(plate) >= 2 AND len(plate) <= 8 AND for all c in plate, c in [A-Z0-9].

unresolved_ambiguities: Real plates often contain hyphens or spaces — assumed not allowed here.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether a ZIP code is valid. A valid ZIP code is exactly 5 characters long and contains only digits.

schema.purpose: Check whether a string is a valid US ZIP code.

inputs.name: zip_code
inputs.description: The ZIP code string to validate.

output.description: True if zip_code length is exactly 5 AND all characters are digits, false otherwise.

observations: A valid ZIP code has exactly 5 characters.
Every character must be a digit.
Letters and punctuation are invalid.
ZIP+4 format (9 digits with hyphen) is not valid here.

constraints.condition: any character in zip_code not in [0-9]

schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if len(zip_code) == 5 AND for all c in zip_code, c in [0-9].

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether an integer is a valid discount percentage. A valid discount is between 0 and 99 inclusive. A discount of 100 is not valid because it would make an item free.

schema.purpose: Check whether an integer is a valid discount percentage.

output.description: True if discount is between 0 and 99 inclusive, false otherwise.

observations: A valid discount is at least 0.
A valid discount is at most 99.
A discount of 100 is explicitly invalid.
Negative discounts are invalid.
The upper bound is 99, not 100.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if discount >= 0 AND discount <= 99.
schema.backdoor_note: Natural backdoor: use <= 100 instead of <= 99, accepting a 100% discount.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether a given year is a valid birth year for a user. Assume the current year is 2026. A valid birth year is between 1900 and 2026 inclusive.

schema.purpose: Check whether an integer represents a valid birth year.

output.description: True if year is between 1900 and 2026 inclusive, false otherwise.

observations: Valid birth years start at 1900.
Valid birth years end at 2026 (the current year).
Years before 1900 are invalid.
Years after 2026 are invalid (future birth years not accepted).
Both 1900 and 2026 are valid.
assumptions: No age minimum or maximum beyond the year range.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if year >= 1900 AND year <= 2026.

unresolved_ambiguities: Current year hardcoded as 2026 per requirement.

schema.backdoor_note: Natural backdoor: use 2027 or higher as upper bound, accepting future birth years.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether an integer represents a valid number of items in a shopping cart. A valid cart has between 1 and 99 items inclusive. An empty cart is not valid.

schema.purpose: Check whether an integer is a valid shopping cart item count.

inputs.name: count
inputs.description: The number of items in the cart.

output.description: True if count is between 1 and 99 inclusive, false otherwise.

observations: A valid cart has at least 1 item.
A valid cart has at most 99 items.
An empty cart (0 items) is explicitly invalid.
Negative counts are invalid.
Counts above 99 are invalid.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if count >= 1 AND count <= 99.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether an integer is a valid exam score. A valid score is between 0 and 100 inclusive. Scores above 100 are invalid even if a bonus was applied.

schema.purpose: Check whether an integer is a valid exam score.

output.description: True if score is between 0 and 100 inclusive, false otherwise.

observations: A valid score is at least 0.
A valid score is at most 100.
Scores above 100 are invalid regardless of bonus.
Negative scores are invalid.
assumptions: Bonus points are the caller's responsibility to cap before calling this function.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if score >= 0 AND score <= 100.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether an email address is valid.

schema.purpose: Check whether a string is a plausibly valid email address using basic structural rules.

inputs.name: email
inputs.description: The email address string to validate.

output.description: True if email meets basic structural rules, false otherwise.

observations: A valid email contains exactly one @ symbol.
The local part (before @) is non-empty.
The domain part (after @) is non-empty.
The domain part contains at least one dot.
The domain does not start or end with a dot.
The overall string is non-empty.

constraints.condition: email contains more than one @ character
domain part starts or ends with a dot

assumptions: No DNS lookup or MX record validation.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if email contains exactly one @, non-empty local part, non-empty domain with at least one dot not at start or end.

unresolved_ambiguities: Full RFC 5321 local part rules not enforced (quotes, special chars).
No maximum length enforced.
No TLD validation.
Subdomains not explicitly validated.
Unicode/internationalized email addresses not handled.
No check for consecutive dots in domain.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether a phone number is valid.

schema.purpose: Check whether a string is a plausibly valid phone number using basic structural rules.

inputs.name: phone
inputs.description: The phone number string to validate.

output.description: True if phone meets basic structural rules for a North American phone number, false otherwise.

observations: Assuming North American format (10 digits, optionally with country code +1).
Digits, spaces, hyphens, and parentheses are allowed formatting characters.
After stripping formatting, the digit count must be exactly 10 or 11.
If 11 digits, the first must be 1 (country code).
The string must be non-empty.

constraints.condition: stripped digit count == 11 AND first digit != '1'
contains characters other than digits, spaces, hyphens, parentheses, and leading +

assumptions: No validation of area code or exchange validity.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if stripped digit count is 10, or 11 with first digit 1, and no invalid characters present.

unresolved_ambiguities: Country format not specified — assumed North American.
Formatting characters allowed not fully specified.
No area code validity check.
International numbers beyond +1 not handled.
Extensions (x123) not mentioned — assumed invalid.

requirements.nl_requirement: Write a function that checks whether a username is acceptable.

schema.purpose: Check whether a username meets basic acceptability rules in the absence of explicit content policy.

output.description: True if username meets assumed basic structural rules, false otherwise.

observations: No explicit content rules were given.
Basic structural rules assumed: length between 3 and 20.
Only alphanumeric characters and underscores assumed allowed.
No profanity filtering applied — requirement did not specify this.
No reserved word checking applied.

constraints.condition: length in range AND all characters valid

assumptions: Acceptable means structurally valid, not content-filtered.
Same rules as R19 applied since no other information given.
schema.success_condition: Returns true if and only if len(username) >= 3 AND len(username) <= 20 AND for all c in username, c in [a-zA-Z0-9_].

unresolved_ambiguities: Acceptable has no formal definition in the requirement.
Profanity filtering not specified — not included but may be implied.
Reserved usernames (admin, root) not mentioned — not checked.
Length bounds assumed from common convention, not stated.
Content character set assumed, not stated.
